commit.message:
fix: new Function() with parameters passes all args to constructor

The compiler's special case for `new Function(...)` only compiled
the last argument (body), discarding parameter arguments. This caused
`new Function('a', 'return a;')` to produce a function with no
parameters (length=0) that couldn't access its arguments.

Fix: compile all arguments and pass them to the native constructor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: yield* return delegation with CheckGeneratorReturn/SetGeneratorReturn opcodes

- Add CheckGeneratorReturn (106) and SetGeneratorReturn (107) opcodes
- Implement spec §14.4.7 step 5.c for yield* return delegation
- Skip catch body when generator_return_pending is set (try/catch/finally)
- Reorder resume_generator: yield takes precedence over pending return
  so yield* can re-yield when inner iterator's .return() reports done=false
- Clear generator_return_pending on YieldDirect yields (delegation handles it)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: javascript
repository.description: A JavaScript engine implementation written in Rust

commit.message:
fix: yield* delegation — use globals for temp vars, fix catch binding, add YieldDirect opcode

- Add YieldDirect opcode (105) that yields inner iterator's result object
  directly without wrapping in {value, done} — implements spec §14.4.7
  step 7.a.vii GeneratorYield(innerResult)
- Use DefineGlobal for yield* temp variables (ys_iter, ys_recv, ys_result,
  ys_thrown) instead of local stack slots to avoid stack corruption when
  multiple yield* expressions appear sequentially in a generator
- Fix catch binding: handle_throw writes to globals but emit_define_var
  created a local slot that shadowed the global, causing thrown values
  to read as undefined — now ys_thrown uses global storage consistently
- Use GetMethod opcode for iterator acquisition (handles primitives)
- Add @@sym:1 (Symbol.iterator) to GetMethod string handler
- Add AssertIterResult validation after iter.next() and iter.throw()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: super property set writes to receiver, not super_base

Per ES spec §9.1.9 OrdinarySet, super.x = val and super[k] = val
should write data properties on the receiver (this), not the
super_base (prototype). This fixes:
- Frozen receiver TypeError for super property writes
- Object.setPrototypeOf accepting VmNativeFunction as prototype

Added lookup_setter_in_chain() helper to separate setter lookup
from data property write target.

commit.message:
fix: comparison operator LeftFirst evaluation order and Symbol TypeError

- Add left_first parameter to abstract_relational_comparison per
  §7.2.14 IsLessThan to ensure ToPrimitive is called on the left
  operand first regardless of argument order.
- Throw TypeError when comparing Symbols (ToNumeric on Symbol).

Fixes 4 more test262 tests (A2.3_T1 eval order, bigint-and-symbol).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: javascript
repository.description: A JavaScript engine implementation written in Rust

commit.message:
fix: implement Abstract Relational Comparison per spec §7.2.14

Rewrite all four comparison operators (<, >, <=, >=) to follow the
spec's Abstract Relational Comparison algorithm:

1. Call ToPrimitive(x, number) on both operands — this invokes
   valueOf/toString on objects.
2. If both are strings, do lexicographic comparison.
3. BigInt vs String: parse string via StringToBigInt (with 0x/0o/0b).
4. ToNumeric on both sides, then handle same-type and cross-type
   (BigInt vs Number) comparisons properly.

Previously the fallback was just to_number() on both sides, which
skipped valueOf calls entirely and didn't handle BigInt cross-type.

Fixes 46 test262 tests in comparison operator clusters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sync class expr name/ref stacks for nested class extends

When an unnamed class expression was compiled, it pushed to
current_class_expr_refs but not current_class_expr_names, causing the
two parallel stacks to become misaligned. This meant that when resolving
a class name reference (e.g. 'Outer') inside a method body containing a
nested class expression (e.g. 'class extends Outer {}'), the compiler
would use .last() on both stacks and pick the wrong temp binding
(__cls_expr_1__ instead of __cls_expr_0__), making the inner class
constructor share the outer class's prototype.

Fix:
- Always push/pop both stacks in sync (empty string for unnamed classes)
- Use rposition indexed lookup in Expr::Var to find the matching name
  and retrieve the corresponding ref by index

Also removes debug eprintln statements from vm.rs and runner.rs.

Fixes 5 class elements tests:
- privatefieldget-success-1.js
- privatefieldget-typeerror-3.js
- privatefieldget-typeerror-5.js
- privatefieldset-typeerror-3.js
- privatefieldset-typeerror-5.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Treat missing timesince cache as cache miss, not error (#94)

First-time `etu` CLI usage logs a confusing error because
`cacheFromFile()` treats a nonexistent cache file as an error rather
than a cache miss.

```
etu: reading timesince cache: open /Users/nat/Library/Application Support/etu/timesince.cache: no such file or directory
```

- `cacheFromFile()` now returns `(nil, nil)` on `os.IsNotExist`, letting
`TimeSinceLastPost` fall through to `UpdateCache` which creates the
directory and file via `MkdirAll`
- Updated `TestCacheFromFileMissing` to assert no error on missing file

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: icco <20201+icco@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: etu
repository.description: A wiki and productivity recorder

fix: BoxLocal must persist cell at top-level for class heritage scope

BoxLocal is only emitted for class-expression name pre-boxing (heritage
scope). The shared cell must persist in top_level_cells so that closures
in the heritage expression and the later SetLocal share the same binding.

The previous commit (3428cb5) gated BoxLocal on is_module_mode to fix
for-of per-iteration binding regressions, but BoxLocal is never emitted
for loop variables — only MakeClosure's auto-created cells needed the
gate. Removing the is_module_mode guard from BoxLocal fixes class
heritage name capture without reintroducing the for-of regression.

Fixes: scope-name-lex-open-heritage.js
test-vm.sh: 100/100

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: receiver namespace TDZ check in OrdinarySetWithOwnDescriptor

When super.foo = V is used and the Receiver is a module namespace exotic
object, spec OrdinarySetWithOwnDescriptor step 2c calls
Receiver.[[GetOwnProperty]](P) which triggers [[Get]] on the namespace.
If the binding is in TDZ (Uninitialized), this must throw ReferenceError.

The check is only performed when no accessor (setter) is found on the
prototype chain — when a setter exists, it is called directly and
Receiver.[[GetOwnProperty]] is never invoked (per spec step 3).

Extracted check_receiver_namespace_tdz_set() helper used in both the
proxy-proto and main property-set paths.

Fixes: super-access-to-tdz-binding.js (ReferenceError for TDZ)
Fixes: super-set-to-tdz-binding-with-accessor.js (setter called, no TDZ)

test262 module clusters: 494 pass, 0 fail
test-vm.sh: 100/100

commit.message:
fix: top_level_cells leak across block scopes in script mode

Gate top_level_cells insertion on is_module_mode in both MakeClosure
and BoxLocal handlers. In script mode, closures now capture by value
(own cell per closure), restoring correct per-iteration binding for
for-of/for-in with const/let and proper TDZ enforcement when local
indices are reused across block scopes.

The regression was introduced by the live import bindings feature
(32df7df) which shared cells via top_level_cells for all top-level
captures. This is only needed in module mode for live bindings.

Fixes: classes_usage.js TDZ test, statement_tests.js const for-of

commit.message:
fix: async module suspension for top-level-await interleaving

Implement module-level await suspension so sibling dependencies can
evaluate while an async module is waiting. Per spec §16.2.1.5.2.1
(InnerModuleEvaluation step 14), when a module hits top-level await,
its execution suspends and sibling modules in the dependency graph
proceed. After all siblings complete, the suspended module resumes.

Added SuspendedModuleState struct to capture VM execution state at the
point of suspension (IP, module_locals, stack, chunk metadata). The
run_opcode_await handler detects module-level await (no call frames)
and exits early when suspend_on_module_await is set. After the dep
loop, suspended modules are resumed in order.

Test results: 492 pass, 0 fail across all 5 module test clusters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: circular re-export fixup pass for module dependency cycles

- Track all re-export dependencies (Named, Star, Namespace) per module
- After loading all modules, run fixup pass that iterates until stable,
  re-resolving re-exports using fully populated module exports
- Fixes circular star re-export cases where module A re-exports from B
  and B re-exports from A (both now see each other's exports)
- Fixes circular named re-export resolution through star export chains
- Preserve ambiguous key exclusion in fixup pass to prevent re-adding
  keys that were correctly identified as ambiguous

commit.message:
Implement Module Namespace Exotic Object semantics (§10.4.6)

- Add cached namespace object with identity preservation via module_locals
- Implement live binding lookups through __ns_bindings__ map for [[Get]], [[GetOwnProperty]], enumeration
- Add namespace [[Get]] handler in run_opcode_get_property for direct property access
- Implement [[Set]] always-false behavior with TypeError in strict mode
- Implement [[Delete]] for exports (non-configurable), symbols, and non-exported keys
- Add namespace handling to DeleteIndex opcode for computed property deletion
- Implement [[HasProperty]] via __ns_bindings__ for both in-operator and Reflect.has
- Implement [[DefineOwnProperty]] no-op validation for Object.defineProperty and Reflect.defineProperty
- Implement [[SetPrototypeOf]] null-only restriction for Object/Reflect.setPrototypeOf
- Implement [[GetOwnProperty]] with live binding ReferenceError on uninitialized
- Implement [[OwnPropertyKeys]] returning sorted export names from bindings
- Add Object.freeze TypeError for namespace objects
- Set anonymous function/generator name to 'default' for export default expressions
- Fix non-configurable property delete regression in regular objects
- Null prototype, non-extensible, Symbol.toStringTag='Module' on namespace objects

commit.message:
build(deps): bump node-forge from 1.3.3 to 1.4.0 (#166) Bumps [node-forge](https://github.com/digitalbazaar/forge) from 1.3.3 to 1.4.0.
Changelog

Sourced from node-forge's changelog.

1.4.0 - 2026-03-24

Security

  • HIGH: Denial of Service in BigInteger.modInverse()
    • A Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability exists due to an infinite loop in the BigInteger.modInverse() function (inherited from the bundled jsbn library). When modInverse() is called with a zero value as input, the internal Extended Euclidean Algorithm enters an unreachable exit condition, causing the process to hang indefinitely and consume 100% CPU.
    • Reported by Kr0emer.
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33891
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-5gfm-wpxj-wjgq
  • HIGH: Signature forgery in RSA-PKCS due to ASN.1 extra field.
    • RSASSA PKCS#1 v1.5 signature verification accepts forged signatures for low public exponent keys (e=3). Attackers can forge signatures by stuffing "garbage" bytes within the ASN.1 structure in order to construct a signature that passes verification, enabling Bleichenbacher style forgery. This issue is similar to CVE-2022-24771, but adds bytes in an addition field within the ASN.1 structure, rather than outside of it.
    • Additionally, forge does not validate that signatures include a minimum of 8 bytes of padding as defined by the specification, providing attackers additional space to construct Bleichenbacher forgeries.
    • Reported as part of a U.C. Berkeley security research project by:
      • Austin Chu, Sohee Kim, and Corban Villa.
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33894
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-ppp5-5v6c-4jwp
  • HIGH: Signature forgery in Ed25519 due to missing S < L check.
    • Ed25519 signature verification accepts forged non-canonical signatures where the scalar S is not reduced modulo the group order (S >= L). A valid signature and its S + L variant both verify in forge, while Node.js crypto.verify (OpenSSL-backed) rejects the S + L variant, as defined by the specification. This class of signature malleability has been exploited in practice to bypass authentication and authorization logic (see CVE-2026-25793, CVE-2022-35961). Applications relying on signature uniqueness (i.e., dedup by signature bytes, replay tracking, signed-object canonicalization checks) may be bypassed.
    • Reported as part of a U.C. Berkeley security research project by:
      • Austin Chu, Sohee Kim, and Corban Villa.
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33895
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-q67f-28xg-22rw
  • HIGH: basicConstraints bypass in certificate chain verification.
    • pki.verifyCertificateChain() does not enforce RFC 5280 basicConstraints requirements when an intermediate certificate lacks both the basicConstraints and keyUsage extensions. This allows any leaf certificate (without these extensions) to act as a CA and sign other certificates, which node-forge will accept as valid.
    • Reported by Doruk Tan Ozturk (@​peaktwilight) - doruk.ch
    • CVE ID: CVE-2026-33896
    • GHSA ID: GHSA-2328-f5f3-gj25

... (truncated)

Commits
  • fa385f9 Release 1.4.0.
  • 07d4e16 Update changelog.
  • cb90fd9 Update changelog.
  • 963e7c5 Add unit test for "pseudonym"
  • f0b6f5b Add pseudonym OID
  • 3df48a3 Fix missing CVE ID.
  • 2e49283 Add x509 basicConstraints check.
  • bdecf11 Add canonical signature scaler check for S < L.
  • af094e6 Add RSA padding and DigestInfo length checks.
  • 796eeb1 Improve jsbn fix.
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) You can disable automated security fix PRs for this repo from the [Security Alerts page](https://github.com/sbroenne/office-coding-agent/network/alerts).

commit.message:
fix: use absolute path for Copilot CLI in container

The SDK's cliPath: 'copilot' fails because /usr/local/bin isn't
reliably on PATH inside the container when run as a non-root user.
Use the absolute path /usr/local/bin/copilot instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: NanoPilot nanopilot-discord channel

commit.message:
fix: exclude test files from agent-runner source cache

The container-runner copies agent-runner/src/ into per-group session
directories. Test files were included, causing tsc to fail inside the
container at runtime. Filter out *.test.ts from the copy.

Also removes stale test files from existing session caches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement module-scoped variable isolation and export hoisting

- Add module_locals IndexMap to VM for module-mode top-level bindings
- Set is_module_mode flag in set_module_this() so declarations don't leak to globalThis
- Modify DefineGlobal/DefineGlobalConst/DefineGlobalSoft to use module_locals in module mode
- Modify GetGlobal/SetGlobal/TypeOfGlobal/DeleteGlobal to check module_locals first
- Add Export wrapper handling in collect_function_var_names_from_statement
- Add Export wrapper handling in emit_hoisted_global_lexicals for let/const/class/destructuring
- Add export function declaration hoisting in top-level compile pass
- Fix class declarations to use mutable bindings at top level (per §14.7.14)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix generator prototype constructor, symbol equality, and constructor backlink guards

- Skip constructor property on generator/async-generator prototype in get_fn_props (spec §27.3.3.1)
- Skip constructor re-add in MakeClosure opcode for generator functions
- Remove constructor from async generator per-instance prototype (spec §27.4.3.1)
- Add contains_key("constructor") guard in read_named_property_with_receiver for VmObject, VmFunction, VmClosure paths to prevent auto-adding constructor to prototypes that never had one
- Fix loose equality for Symbol vs Object: call ToPrimitive on the non-symbol side before comparing (spec §7.2.14 step 10)
- Flatten nested if-let chains in compiler for generator/async-generator named expression self-reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
